Mining Technical and Financial Advisory | Client-Facing Consulting Role

Own the analysis. Stand behind the numbers. Advise the decision.

Xenith is a leading consulting firm supporting the Resources, Energy and Infrastructure sectors across the full asset lifecycle. Our Advisory team works at the intersection of technical, financial and strategic decision-making — supporting corporates, investors and financiers on critical transactions and investments.

We are seeking a Principal Analyst – Consulting to strengthen our senior analytical capability and provide trusted, defensible advice to clients across the resources sector.

This is a senior, hands-on role for a highly analytical professional who is comfortable owning complex financial and technical analysis, engaging directly with clients, and contributing to high-value investment and transaction decisions.

The Opportunity

Based in Sydney, this role is suited to an experienced mining, investment or financial advisory professional operating at Principal level.

You will work across a diverse portfolio of projects supporting mining companies, financial institutions, private equity, legal and accounting firms.

You will:

• Lead and deliver high-quality analytical work across consulting engagements

• Act as a trusted advisor to clients at technical, commercial and executive level

• Take ownership of complex models, assumptions and conclusions

• Provide senior input into transactions, valuations and investment decisions

• Mentor junior analysts and lift analytical standards across the team

This is not a coordination or reporting-only role. You will remain close to the analysis, the detail and the decisions.

Key Responsibilities

Analytical Leadership & Advisory Delivery

• Lead and review complex financial, technical and commercial analysis

• Develop and interrogate financial models for mining and resource assets

• Apply strong judgement to assumptions, sensitivities and scenarios

• Ensure analytical outputs are robust, defensible and decision-ready




Transaction & Investment Support

• Support M&A transactions and investment decisions

• Deliver technical due diligence and expert assessments

• Contribute to mineral asset valuations and investment evaluations

• Support lenders, investors and boards with clear, evidence-based advice




Client Advisory & Communication

• Act as a client-facing advisor, presenting findings clearly and confidently

• Translate complex technical and financial analysis into clear insights

• Prepare high-quality reports, expert opinions and advisory papers

• Support expert witness work and independent advisory engagements


Mentoring & Capability Building

• Mentor analysts and consultants across the Advisory team

• Lift analytical rigour, modelling standards and commercial thinking

• Contribute to internal methodologies, templates and best practice




About You

You are an analytical, commercially astute professional who thrives in complex problem-solving environments.

You have experience as a professional analyst in or serving the mining and resources industry, preferably underpinned by demonstrable experience in a technical discipline.

You are comfortable standing behind your work and explaining it to sophisticated stakeholders.

You thrive in a client serving, demanding and dynamic consulting environment.


Skills, Qualifications & Experience

• Degree in Mining Engineering, Finance, Economics, Geology or related discipline

• Typically, 5–10+ years’ experience in mining, investment, financial or technical advisory

• Exceptional analytical skills with strong attention to detail

• Advanced financial modelling and analytical capability

• Experience across mining projects, transactions or resource investments

• Strong written and verbal communication skills

• Comfortable working in a client-facing consulting environment

• Commercially aware, pragmatic and outcome-focused




Why Xenith

• High-trust, technically strong consulting culture

• Exposure to complex, high-impact advisory work

• Flexible working arrangements

• Competitive remuneration aligned to role and accountability

From $152,074 - $174,312 (depending on experience) + 15.4% superannuation

The team

The People and Culture (P&C) team supports ASIC's people by fostering an inclusive, engaging workplace and driving professional growth. Its focus includes talent acquisition, learning and development, performance management, and organisational development, alongside promoting diversity, equity and equal opportunity.

 

The role

The role focuses on engaging stakeholders at all levels, executing practical plans that deliver measurable benefits, and supporting communications for key P&C initiatives and campaigns.

Contribute to enterprise-wide change management roadmap and maturity uplift.
Collaborate with Digital, Data and Technology to standardise tools and approaches.
Support broader P&C business change activities and capability building (including training).
Drive a culture of innovation, continuous improvement, and adoption of change.

 

About you

Qualifications & Experience:

Demonstrated strength in delivering strategic change, ideally in large-scale cultural or people technology initiatives.
Experience working with the implementation of an HR software system within the public sector highly regarded.
Proven capability working in complex enterprise level environments.
Ability to plan and manage people, process, and behavioural change activities.
Understanding of modern web technologies and publishing processes.
Expertise in developing and delivering complex content across digital and traditional channels.
Strong written and verbal communication, including executive-level reporting.

About AARNet

Australia’s Academic and Research Network (AARNet), founded in 1989, has played a pivotal role in shaping Australia’s digital landscape as the pioneers of the country’s Internet. Our mission extends beyond mere connectivity; we are the architects, builders, and operators of a world-class network infrastructure that fuels critical research and learning. At AARNet, innovation thrives within a vibrant community.




The Role: Senior Commercial Analyst

We are seeking a high-calibre Senior Commercial Analyst to support major infrastructure and construction projects through robust financial analysis, cost modelling, and commercial insight.

The role will play a key part in developing business cases, project forecasts, bid models, and executive reporting, with a particular focus on infrastructure cost modelling, including P50 / P80 estimates, contingency analysis, and risk-adjusted forecasting. 

Key Responsibilities include:

Commercial and Financial Analysis

Develop, maintain and enhance commercial models for major infrastructure and construction projects.
Prepare cost models, forecasts, cash flow projections, margin analysis, and scenario modelling.
Support business cases, pricing submissions, and executive reporting.
Analyse project performance, forecast accuracy, and key commercial risks.  

Infrastructure Cost Modelling

Build and review infrastructure cost estimates across project lifecycle stages.
Apply risk-based cost modelling techniques and probabilistic estimating methodologies.
Develop P50 / P80 and other confidence-level cost positions to support investment, contingency, and delivery decisions.
Ensure cost models are transparent, defensible, and suitable for governance review.

Commercial Governance and Reporting

Support project and portfolio reporting, including forecast updates, variance analysis, risks, opportunities, and key financial commentary.
Assist in preparing reports and presentations for senior management and Boards.  

Bid and Delivery Support

Provide commercial input into tenders, bids, proposals, and customer pricing.
Assess delivery risks, margin sensitivity, and downside / upside scenarios.
Contribute to negotiations by providing structured financial and risk analysis.   




Skills and Experience Essential

Relevant degrees in Commerce, Finance, Economics, Engineering, Quantity Surveying, Construction Management, or similar.
Experience in commercial, financial analysis, project controls, or infrastructure finance role.
Experience supporting major infrastructure, construction, engineering, utilities, or telecommunications projects.
Strong experience developing project cost models, including risk-adjusted and probabilistic estimates.
Proven experience working with P50 / P80 estimates, confidence-based forecasting, contingency modelling, and cost risk analysis.
Advanced financial modelling capability in Excel and strong analytical skills.
Experience in business case development, project forecasting, and commercial reporting.
Strong communication skills, with the ability to present complex analysis clearly to senior stakeholders. 

Desirable

Experience in PPP, transport, utilities, fibre, energy, civil, or large-scale construction environments.
Experience using project controls, estimating, or risk tools such as Power BI, Primavera, @Risk, Cleopatra, EcoSys, or similar.
CPA, CA, CFA or postgraduate commercial / finance qualifications are advantageous.   




Why Work at AARNet?

At AARNet, we’re passionate about advancing global education and critical research. Our Australiawide network, with global reach, supports some of the most demanding networkbased applications. If you’re looking for a purpose-driven workplace where you can make a difference, consider joining our team.

Make an impact: By working with us, you’ll directly contribute to critical research in fields like astronomy, physics, earth observation, environment, health, and medicine. Our mission matters, and we’re committed to providing equal opportunities for all.  




Competitive Benefits and Conditions:

Equal Opportunity Focus: We value diversity and inclusion.
Social Responsibility and Ethics: We lead by example.
Cutting-Edge Technologies: Access to work with the latest industry technology.

Job Title: UKG Pro Specialists (Solutions Architect/Project Analyst/HRIS Reporting Officer)
Company: Talent – Specialists in tech, transformation & beyond
Ad Text:
The Opportunity

A large, purpose-driven health services organisation is investing in the capability of its HRIS and Workforce Management platforms - and is looking to bring on three experienced specialists to support this critical program of work. At the heart of this initiative is UKG Pro: two of the three roles are dedicated to maximising the platform's potential, from high-level solution architecture through to day-to-day system support and reporting. The third role anchors the broader HRIS function, ensuring the integrity and performance of SAP/SuccessFactors and Kronos environments that underpin payroll, compliance, and workforce data across the organisation. Together, these roles form a cohesive, high-impact team - and represent a genuine opportunity to leave a lasting footprint in a complex, regulated environment.

Role 1 | Solutions Architect - UKG Pro WFM

This is the most senior of the three roles and the linchpin of the UKG Pro investment. The Solutions Architect owns the design, configuration, and integration of the UKG Pro Workforce Management platform across the enterprise - covering Timekeeper, Accruals, Scheduling, Leave, and Attendance. With deep technical expertise and a strong commercial mindset, you will lead enterprise integrations via Dell Boomi, drive process automation, and ensure all WFM solutions are compliant, scalable, and operationally sound. This role requires someone who can operate confidently at both architecture and delivery level, engaging senior stakeholders while guiding junior team members.

Key Requirements

5-7+ years in UKG Pro WFM or UKG Dimensions in a solution architecture or functional lead capacity, with multi-company implementation experience.
Expertise across UKG Pro WFM modules (Timekeeper, Accruals, Scheduling, Leave) and hands-on experience with integration platforms such as Dell Boomi.
Strong stakeholder engagement and documentation skills; UKG Pro WFM certifications and health sector experience are highly regarded.

Role 2 | UKG Pro Project Analyst (SME)

Complementing the Solutions Architect, the UKG Pro Project Analyst serves as the operational backbone of the UKG Pro platform - the person the business turns to for day-to-day system support, configuration, reporting, and troubleshooting. This role is critical to maintaining data accuracy and platform integrity across HR and Payroll functions, and to translating evolving business needs into system improvements. If you have strong UKG Pro module knowledge, a data-driven mindset, and enjoy working at the intersection of technology and people operations, this role will keep you engaged and challenged throughout.

Key Requirements

Demonstrated UKG Pro experience in an analyst or support capacity, with strong knowledge of core modules and reporting tools including Excel and Power BI.
Solid understanding of HR and payroll processes, data governance, and system dependencies within a complex, multi-site environment.
Tertiary qualifications in HR, Information Systems, or Business; experience in healthcare or a regulated environment is an advantage.

Role 3 | HRIS Applications Reporting Officer

While the two UKG Pro roles focus on WFM platform advancement, the HRIS Applications Reporting Officer keeps the broader HRIS ecosystem running cleanly. Working primarily across SAP/SuccessFactors and Kronos, this role is the key contact point for data extraction, reporting, compliance submissions, and application support across HR, Payroll, Recruitment, and Finance. Payroll accuracy, Single Touch Payroll compliance, and clean data feeds to external agencies all depend on the person in this role doing their job well - making it an essential piece of the organisation's operational foundation.

Key Requirements

3+ years in HRIS operations and business analysis, with hands-on proficiency in SAP/SuccessFactors and Kronos, and advanced Excel and Power BI skills.
Strong payroll knowledge including awards, EBAs, STP compliance, and experience managing data flows to ATO, superannuation funds, and external agencies.
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills; experience in a training or user-support capacity within healthcare or a regulated environment is desirable.

What's in it for You

Initial 3-Month Contracts with a strong chance to extend.
Prahran, Melbourne location with hybrid working arrangements.
Be part of a collaborative, high-performing HRIS and WFM team committed to platform excellence, compliance, and continuous improvement.

Company description:



Newmont is the world's leading gold company and a producer of copper, silver, zinc, lead and molybdenum. The company's world-class portfolio of assets, prospects and talent is anchored in favourable mining jurisdictions in Australia, Papua New Guinea, Africa, Latin America & the Caribbean and North America. Newmont is the only gold producer listed in the S&P 500 Index and is widely recognized for its principled environmental, social and governance practices. The company is an industry leader in value creation, supported by robust safety standards, superior execution and technical proficiency. Newmont was founded in 1921 and has been publicly traded since 1925.

At Newmont, people create the value in the metals we mine responsibly and are ambassadors in the communities where they live and work. As a team, we foster an environment in support of our people vision, where everyone belongs, thrives and is valued. We have built a diverse and inclusive workplace where our people can bring their differences to support delivering results together.

Newmont APAC Operations are located in Western Australia, the Northern Territory, New South Wales and Papua New Guinea, with roles in operations, projects, corporate support and exploration. APAC Business Unit Support is provided from our offices in Brisbane, Subiaco and Melbourne.

Join us and unearth your potential in a global company that is at the forefront of shaping the future of the mining industry. Location negotiable.



Job description:



Who we are looking for!

As a Senior Analyst (BA) you will support the delivery of high-value data initiatives across Newmont's global operations, with a focus on the processing domain. Acting as a key bridge between business stakeholders and technical teams, you will translate operational needs into scalable, data driven solutions through strong stakeholder alignment, problem definition, and data readiness. Leveraging experience in mining, metallurgy, manufacturing, or resources, you will apply a practical, analytical approach to deliver insights that driver operational excellence and continuous improvement in complex, cross-functional environments.

This position is best suited to experienced practitioners with a proven track record of delivering outcomes in complex, cross-functional, industrial environments, not early career professionals.

Key Work Activities

Translating business needs into clear data and analytic requirements as the link between stakeholders and technical teams.
Partnering with Data Engineering and AI/ML teams to design and deliver fit-for-purpose data solutions.
Contributing to development of processing data products aligned to operational and metallurgical requirements.
Supporting implementation and adoption of new data processes and technologies across operational environments.

Your new role


The focus of this role is to work closely with the Quant and Risk Function to support the wider advisory business. This opportunity is best suited to an analytically oriented and motivated candidate with strong technical skills to help develop and support the quantitative tools and models that JANA uses to undertake investment research, formulate investment advice, and provide analysis and reporting. 


This role can be based in either our Sydney or Melbourne office and reports to the Head of Quant and Risk.

Develop, maintain, and enhance quantitative models and tools to support investment research, portfolio construction, and risk analysis
Analyse portfolio performance, attribution, and risk across multi‑asset portfolios, including equities, fixed income, cash, and derivatives.
Build and manage data pipelines and database structures using internal and external data sources (e.g. market and vendor data)
Apply statistical techniques to large and complex datasets to generate insights, reports and dashboards for internal and external stakeholders
Create and maintain programmatic models to deliver systematic and repeatable analysis
Support the development of analytics and frameworks for performance attribution, risk monitoring and portfolio evaluation

Position Description:


We are seeking a versatile Senior Data Scientist who can work across applied analytics and machine learning domains, including computer vision, statistical analysis, and policy‑relevant insight generation. This role is ideal for a data scientist who enjoys balancing exploratory analysis, model development, and clear communication of findings to technical and non‑technical audiences.
You will work with multidisciplinary teams including data engineers, BI developers, architects, and business stakeholders. You will focus on analysis, modeling, experimentation, and insight generation.
This position supports a wide range of analytics and AI initiatives such as computer vision prototyping, urban and regulatory analytics, housing and economic trend analysis, and other data‑driven decision‑support projects.

Your future duties and responsibilities:


. Conduct statistical, time‑series, and trend analysis to identify patterns, anomalies, changes over time, and emerging signals.
. Develop clear, reproducible analytical methods, KPIs, and metrics used in dashboards and recurring reports.
. Translate analytical outcomes into recommendations, narratives, and visual summaries for stakeholders.
. Support compliance, policy, planning, operational, or performance‑monitoring use cases with data‑driven insights.
. Collaborate with BI developers to ensure analytical logic is accurately reflected in dashboards and reports.

Computer Vision & Machine Learning

. Design and run computer vision experiments (classification, detection, segmentation) using real‑world imagery.
. Handle preprocessing challenges such as variable image quality, occlusions, inconsistent lighting, and diverse viewpoints.
. Develop evaluation methods, dataset documentation, and experiment reproducibility practices.
. Work within modern ML platforms to manage models, experiment tracking, and model artifacts.
. Participate in human‑in‑the‑loop evaluation workflows to validate model performance and support iterative improvement.

Collaboration & Governance

. Work within Azure ML and related Azure services.
. Partner with data engineers, architects, and SMEs to ensure analytical readiness and data quality.
. Produce well‑structured documentation: model cards, metric definitions, analytical notes, and reproducible notebooks.
. Adhere to privacy, security, and Responsible AI practices when handling sensitive or identifiable data.
. Communicate results clearly to diverse technical and non‑technical audiences.

Required qualifications to be successful in this role:


. Bachelor's degree in a quantitative or technical discipline is required.
. Strong analytical reasoning skills, including the ability to translate ambiguous business or policy questions into structured analytical or ML approaches.
. Experience applying modern AI techniques (e.g., embeddings, semantic search, or foundation‑model‑powered analysis) in practical, production‑minded settings.
. Hands‑on experience evaluating or integrating pre‑trained models into analytics or ML workflows using Azure AI services.
. Experience with Azure AI Foundry or similar foundation‑model experimentation environments.
. Demonstrated ability to collaborate effectively with data engineers, BI developers, architects, and domain experts in multidisciplinary teams.
. Experience designing and evaluating experiments, including defining metrics, baselines, sampling strategies, and reproducible evaluation procedures.
. Understanding of Responsible AI principles, risk mitigation, bias evaluation, and explainability best practices.
. 5+ years of experience in applied data science or analytics, producing measurable business or policy impacts.
. Strong Python skills (pandas, numpy, scikit‑learn; plus PyTorch or TensorFlow for ML/CV).
. Strong SQL skills for querying and analytical exploration.
. Ability to communicate complex technical concepts in clear, accessible language.
. Experience working in environments with privacy, security, or auditability requirements.

Preferred Qualifications

. Master's degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Engineering, Statistics, or a related field is preferred
. Experience with cloud‑based ML environments or ML lifecycle tools.
. Experience with geospatial data, coordinate systems, or spatial analysis.
. Familiarity with annotation workflows, labeling QA, or human‑in‑the‑loop processes.
. Background in impact analysis, behavioral pattern detection, or compliance analytics.
. Knowledge of Responsible AI principles and data‑ethics practices.

Gamuda is a global engineering, property and infrastructure company with an excellent track record of delivering world-class major projects. The New South Wales (NSW) Government has appointed Gamuda Engineering, to deliver the AUD2.7 billion Sydney Metro West - Stations Package West, a vital component of the broader Sydney Metro project.

The package contract includes the design and construction of five new metro stations along the Sydney Metro West alignment: Westmead, North Strathfield, Burwood North, Five Dock, and The Bays.

About the Role:

The Sustainability Analyst Lead is a data-driven role that supports the implementation of Gamuda Australia’s Sustainability Management System at the delivery of the Sydney Metro West: Station Package West project. This role leverages advanced data analysis, artificial intelligence (AI), and workflow automation to streamline the tracking and reporting of sustainability metrics. The Analyst will play a critical role in managing the rigorous data required for Green Star verifications and fulfilling Sydney Metro's specific project obligations.

Duties & Responsibilities:

Functional lead for all Sydney Metro sustainability reporting requirements, responsible for designing and automating processes and workflows across the project

Lead the preparation, drafting, and finalisation of sustainability reports required by Sydney Metro, ensuring accuracy, clarity, and compliance with project and client standards.

Establish early-phase data strategies and automated documentation workflows during project mobilisation to reduce manual administrative tasks.

Utilise digital tools to assist in rapidly analysing, tracking, and interpreting complex, client-specific sustainability requirements mandated by Sydney Metro during delivery

Engage with design consultants, subcontractors, and suppliers to integrate their data feeds into a centralized sustainability tracking systems

Qualifications & Experience

Tertiary qualifications in Data Science/Analytics, Business Analyst, Finance, Sustainability, or a related discipline.

3–5+ years’ experience in a data-focused sustainability role, or a strong background in data analytics/engineering with a keen interest to transition into the sustainability sector

Strong technical background with the ability to build automated systems for coordinating inputs, complex data analysis, and sustainability reporting for infrastructure projects

Advanced Excel skills (including pivot tables, macros, and data visualisation), VB Programming Experience

Experience leveraging AI and automation to streamline the tracking of sustainability frameworks (e.g., energy, materials, and water use data, carbon reduction data, social procurement tracking)

Ability to work collaboratively within multidisciplinary project teams (including Digital Engineering teams) to apply systems thinking and automated problem-solving to project delivery.

Why Gamuda:

Gamuda believes in creating a high-performance work culture that’s fair and welcoming to all. Gamuda comes with a wealth of technical expertise, environmental compliance and experience addressing social matters in coordinating and managing complex project interfaces and risks.

Working for Gamuda in Australia means being embedded in major infrastructure delivery, where technical capability, stakeholder management, and sustainability all carry equal weight.

TMGM (TradeMax Global Markets) is a Sydney-headquartered, ASIC-regulated multi-asset FinTech broker and one of the world's leading CFD brokers by trading volume. Founded in 2013 and rebranded to TMGM in 2020, the group today serves clients globally with 12,000+ tradable instruments across forex, indices, metals, energies, equities and crypto — delivered on MT4, MT5 and proprietary platforms.

About the Role


We are looking for a Senior BI Analyst who pairs sharp business thinking with deep, hands-on data skills — someone who does not just build dashboards but actively shapes how the company measures success. You will partner with Trading, Sales, Marketing, Product and Risk to turn complex questions into clean metrics, dashboards and decisions.

This is a high-visibility individual-contributor role with a clear progression path. You will work directly with C-level sponsors on the company's North Star and KPI tree, and there is meaningful room to grow into BI Lead and ultimately Head of BI as the function scales with the business.

Key Responsibilities

Partner with business teams (Sales, Trading, Marketing, Product, Risk) to translate requirements into clean data solutions and decision-ready metrics.
Design, build and maintain Power BI dashboards with rigorous data modelling, performance tuning and version-controlled semantic definitions.
Write complex SQL (joins, window functions, CTEs, performance-aware queries) against large production datasets for extraction, transformation and analysis.
Own business metrics, KPIs and reporting frameworks — from North Star definition to field-level metric dictionaries with clear ownership and versioning.
Conduct deep-dive analysis on customer behaviour, trading activity, funnel conversion and revenue drivers; turn findings into specific, testable recommendations.
Provide actionable insights that directly improve trading volume, client lifetime value, conversion and retention.
Leverage modern AI tools and “vibe coding” workflows to accelerate analysis, prototype dashboards and automate repetitive BI work.
Communicate findings clearly to senior stakeholders and drive execution — you are expected to close the loop, not just present slides.

Requirements

5–8 years in data analysis / BI / analytics roles, with at least 2 years in a Senior IC capacity.
Strong SQL — multi-table joins, window functions, CTEs, aggregations, and comfortable with performance tuning on large fact tables.
Hands-on Power BI at a production level — dashboard design, data modelling (star schema), DAX, RLS, and semantic model governance.
Strong business acumen — you connect numbers to real-world outcomes, not just chart them.
Comfortable using AI tools (Claude, Copilot, ChatGPT) and modern data workflows to multiply your own output.
Excellent written and verbal communication; confident stakeholder management from analyst to C-level.
Strong execution and an ownership mindset — you close loops, push decisions through to action, and measure impact.
Nice to Have

Direct exposure to CFD / MT4 / MT5 data models (trades, positions, P&L, spreads, slippage).
CFA / CFA Level-candidate or a finance-related degree.
Experience with dbt, Snowflake, Databricks or similar modern data stacks.
Prior work on semantic layer / metric dictionary projects.
Exposure to AML / compliance reporting, risk management reporting, or broker P&L attribution.

Benefits

Work on data powering one of the world’s leading CFD trading platforms — with a USD 2.045T monthly average trading volume, a USD 3T record month, and millions of trades processed daily.
Clear progression: Senior BI Analyst → BI Lead → Head of BI. The BI function is scaling — early joiners get the seats closest to the leadership team.
Genuine hybrid model with a structured work-from-home benefit.
Conference, certification and AI-tooling budget — we back people who keep learning.
Power BI + modern data stack + AI-native workflows. No legacy drag.

Job Title: AI Lead – Trading Systems & Agent Development
Ad Text:
Most AI systems never face reality.

Ours do.

We deploy into live trading environments where information is incomplete, conditions change quickly, and performance is measurable in real time.

We are looking for someone to build systems that improve how decisions are made and executed under pressure — systems trusted because they work, not because they sound impressive.

This role carries real ownership, direct commercial impact, and unusually fast feedback loops.

The Opportunity

You will work directly with traders and decision-makers to build AI systems that:
- identify actionable signals faster
- improve decision quality under uncertainty
- compress the time between insight and execution
- increase consistency and execution precision
- continuously improve from live outcomes

You will own systems end-to-end:
problem framing → architecture → deployment → iteration

The expectation is production impact.

What Makes This Role Different

Most AI roles sit far from commercial reality.

This one does not.

Your work will be deployed quickly, tested under live conditions, and measured against outcomes that matter.

You will operate with:
- direct access to decision-makers
- minimal bureaucracy
- high autonomy
- high accountability
- tight feedback loops
- meaningful commercial exposure

This is an environment for builders who want to see their systems used, trusted, and scaled.

What We’re Looking For

You have already built systems that operate reliably in production environments.

You are comfortable working with:
- incomplete information
- imperfect data
- unclear problem definitions
- fast iteration cycles
- high-stakes decision environments

You move quickly.
You think commercially.
You care about measurable outcomes.

You are likely stronger at shipping than presenting.

Technical Background

You will probably have strong experience across several of:
- Python and modern ML infrastructure
- LLM systems and agent architectures
- orchestration, retrieval, and evaluation systems
- reinforcement learning or optimisation systems
- event-driven or streaming systems
- low-latency production environments
- real-time inference infrastructure

Experience in trading, betting, or probabilistic decision systems is useful but not required.

We care more about judgement, speed, and the ability to build systems that survive contact with reality.

What Success Looks Like

Within the first few months, we would expect:
- multiple systems deployed into live workflows
- measurable improvements against baseline performance
- tighter decision loops
- increased execution consistency
- systems trusted by operators in production

Success in this role is measured by commercial impact.

Environment

- Small team
- High trust
- Fast execution
- Direct communication
- High standards
- Strong ownership culture

We hire carefully and prefer a small number of exceptional people over scaling headcount.

Compensation

Compensation is flexible and designed to attract exceptional individuals.

Nice to have: Experience in culture transformation, behavioural change, operating model change, or supporting AI/tech adoption from a human-centred design lens.
Why join us?

Permanent, hybrid role (with potential for a 4-day week for the right candidate) based in Central Sydney.
Meaningful work with progressive clients alongside a talented, multidisciplinary team.
A steep learning curve in a niche space, stretching your craft across culture transformation, performance and AI adoption.
Work that uniquely blends strategy, design, facilitation and human behaviour.
Sound like you?
If you’re excited by designing and facilitating meaningful change, and enjoy turning complex challenges into practical, human-centred solutions, we’d love to hear from you.

What if you could do the kind of work the world needs?

At WSP, you can access our global scale, contribute to landmark projects and connect with the brightest minds in your field to do the best work of your life. You can embrace your curiosity in a culture that celebrates new ideas and diverse perspectives. You can experience a world of opportunity and the chance to shape a career as unique as you.

This role will see you enhance your capabilities and the reputation of WSP’s Mining team. You will collaborate with talented professionals working on iconic projects to drive future ready outcomes that will benefit clients and communities.

At WSP you’ll find the scale and reach you need to do the kind of work the world needs – and the culture and people that make it the best work of your life.

The Role

We are seeking an experienced Study Manager to support our Mining & Metals business in delivering front-end studies and due diligence projects. This role will provide project management and technical coordination across multi-disciplinary teams, working closely with both internal business units and external stakeholders.

You will play a key role in leading and delivering high-quality study outcomes, from early-stage scoping through to Pre-Feasibility and Definitive Feasibility Studies.

Lead and coordinate mining studies and due diligence projects (Scoping, PFS, DFS)

Manage and deliver peer reviews and independent assessments

Coordinate multi-disciplinary proposals for studies and due diligence assignments

Provide leadership to project teams to ensure quality, consistency and timely delivery

Oversee cost estimate reviews and financial model inputs

Manage study scheduling, budgets, and reporting requirements

Drive integration across key technical disciplines
 

About You

Background in Process Engineering (preferred) with exposure to mining study environments

Proven experience delivering front-end studies within mining or heavy industry

Strong ability to lead multi-disciplinary teams and drive outcomes

Commercial awareness and ability to interpret project financials and cost models

Technical Capability

Working knowledge across major mining disciplines to enable effective technical integration

Familiarity with JORC, NI 43-101 and S-K 1300 reporting standards

Understanding of study phase requirements and toll-gate processes

Experience with AUSIMM and AACE cost estimation frameworks

Exposure to;

Commodity markets and off-take arrangements

Cost and commodity benchmarking

Basic financial modelling and project valuation


Why WSP?

Work on major mining projects across Australia and globally

Collaborate with high-performing, multidisciplinary teams

Opportunity to influence early-phase project decisions and strategy

Flexible working environment and strong focus on employee development

If you're looking to step into a role where you can lead studies, influence project direction, and work across the full mining value chain, we’d like to hear from you.

Randstad Sourceright is proud to be partnering with and working alongside a leading Australian financial services organisation dedicated to providing retirement security and excellence in superannuation management.





Are you an analytical and detail-oriented professional passionate about improving data reliability and transparency? We are looking for a Data Business Analyst to join the Transformation and Technology business unit. This is a key role focused on translating business needs into actionable data and system requirements, ensuring our data solutions are accurate, trusted, and aligned with organisational standards. It is an ideal opportunity for a proactive team member who enjoys mapping complex data flows, documenting specifications, and partnering with stakeholders to solve data management issues at the root cause.




Role: Data Business Analyst

Location: Canberra or Sydney (hybrid)

Job Type: contract - confirmed until Dec 26, ongoing after EOFY approval

Start Date: ASAP




Responsibilities:

Requirements & Documentation: Gather and document business requirements and operational processes to support data initiatives, translating business needs into clear specifications for solution design.

Data Analysis & Integrity: Analyse and validate datasets to identify management issues, uncover root causes, and provide recommendations to improve data accuracy and integrity.

Flow Mapping & Lineage: Map end-to-end data flows and lineage across systems to support impact analysis, solution design, and regulatory requirements.

Workshop Facilitation: Lead workshops with business and data owners to define Critical Data Elements (CDEs), data controls, quality thresholds, and data interpretations.

Stakeholder Collaboration: Partner closely with data engineers, architects, and product teams to ensure solutions align with strategic objectives and organisational standards.

Testing & Adoption: Support testing, deployment, and change management by preparing test cases, validating outputs, and ensuring the smooth adoption of new data solutions.

Governance Support: Develop and maintain data-related policies, standards, and procedural documentation.





Experience:

Industry Expertise: Business analysis experience, focused on data management, data governance, or a related discipline.

Industry Knowledge: Experience within the Australian Superannuation or wider Financial Services sector is highly regarded.

Technical Proficiency: Strong skills in writing SQL queries to extract and validate data, proficiency in BI tools (PowerBI/Tableau), and advanced Excel (lookups, pivots, modelling).

Core Competencies: Proven ability to map business workflows (current state → future state) and a solid understanding of data modelling fundamentals, CDE concepts, and metadata.

Communication & Influence: Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to simplify complex data concepts and facilitate productive stakeholder relationships.

Morgan McKinley are partnering exclusively with a Global Bank as they expand their SOX team in Sydney. We're on the hunt for a highly experienced Senior SOX and Risk Analyst to join the Finance team, working Hybrid.

This is a Day Rate Contract running until 29/01/27 but the goal is to convert to a permanent role.

A key part of this role will be operational risk management, looking at ways to improve control and strengthen the risk environment in relation to SOX. You will provide first line support to internal and external stakeholders in response to operational risk and ensure that the finance department is compliant with the Global Bank's standard.

This role would ideally suit an experienced auditor who has had a focus on risk and control around SOX, looking for their next challenge.

Responsibilities:

Develop, implement and maintain the operational risk and governance framework, ensuring internal controls align across the frameworks.
Take ownership of the Controls Scoping, Risk Controls Self Assessments (RCSAs) and Test of Design Walkthroughs. Be the point of contact for all SOX testing, ensuring that all controls are tested regularly and that necessary changes are made.
Provide support to the first line testers and second line monitors from both a process and documentation perspective.
Work closely with external auditors, process owners, senior management and the operational risk teams. Coordinate with offshore counterparts who also conduct a lot of testing for Australia.
Optimise controls from Manual to Automated, identify internal control deficiencies and create and implement corrective actions for processes in SOX scope.

Requirements:

Chartered Accountant with 5-10 years experience working within financial services and operational audits.
Proven experience in a hands-on SOX Compliance role.
Successful project management skills, excellent communication both written and verbal and the ability to work autonomously to deliver within tight timeframes.
Strong analytical and Excel skills, a great attention to detail and a passion for problem solving.
An autonomous worker with confidence in dealing with key stakeholders across different business units to build relationships and influence.

If this opportunity sounds aligned to your career goals, then reach out to Hannah Sykes today for a confidential discussion as the client is looking to interview as soon as possible!

Please only apply if you hold full Australian Working Rights. Only successful candidates will be contacted.

About Us

Biointelect is a leading life science consultancy that partners with global clients to deliver strategic insights and comprehensive solutions across the life science ecosystem.

Our mission, Bringing Innovations to Life, Step by Step, ensures our clients are supported every step of the way, with deep expertise ensuring their life science innovation is ready for successful commercialisation. Our team is dedicated to delivering evidence-based solutions that drive successful market access and patient outcomes.

About the role

As a Senior Manager within Biointelect’s Commercialisation, Access and Policy team, you will play a central role in helping life science innovators navigate the journey from early innovation through to commercial success.

Working across pharmaceutical, biotech and health technology clients, you will lead strategic commercialisation projects spanning opportunity assessment, market access, pricing, launch readiness and broader market strategy. This is a highly collaborative consulting role suited to someone who combines strong strategic capability with real-world industry experience and enjoys working closely with clients to solve complex challenges.

You’ll partner with senior stakeholders, lead multidisciplinary project teams, contribute to business growth through client engagement and opportunity development, and help shape the future of healthcare innovation across Australia and globally.

Key Responsibilities

Lead the development and delivery of commercialisation strategies across the product lifecycle, including market access, pricing and launch readiness
Partner with pharmaceutical, biotech and health technology clients to provide strategic, evidence-based commercial advice
Lead complex consulting projects, ensuring high-quality delivery, stakeholder alignment and project excellence
Build trusted relationships with senior stakeholders and facilitate strategic discussions and workshops
Contribute to business development activities including client briefings, proposal development and converting opportunities into long-term client engagements
Provide leadership, mentoring and development support to direct reports, contributing to a collaborative and high-performing team culture
Apply industry and market insights to support client outcomes, thought leadership and continuous improvement initiatives

To be successful in the role, you’ll have

Significant experience in management consulting, ideally within life sciences, healthcare or pharmaceutical strategy
Direct pharmaceutical or biotech industry experience across commercial, medical, market access, policy or strategic functions will be highly regarded
Demonstrated experience leading complex, multi-stakeholder projects and high-performing teams
Strong strategic thinking and analytical capability, with the ability to translate complexity into actionable insights
Excellent communication, facilitation and stakeholder engagement skills
A collaborative leadership style with a genuine passion for mentoring and developing others
A strong understanding of the Australian healthcare and life sciences environment

Why Join Biointelect?

You’ll join a purpose‑driven organisation that sits at the intersection of science, strategy and impact. You’ll work alongside and lead industry experts on cutting-edge projects that shape the future of health technology. We value innovation, strategic thinking, and a commitment to improving patient outcomes. This is a unique opportunity to utilise your consulting career in a team that blends scientific rigour with commercial strategy.

If you’re passionate about life sciences and want to make an impact, we welcome your application.

Please include your resume and a brief cover letter outlining your suitability for the role.

Biointelect is an equal opportunity employer. All applicants will be considered for employment without attention to race, colour, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran, or disability status.

The  Australian Health Service Alliance  acts on behalf of 20 small to medium private health funds across Australia. They manage provider relationships, negotiate contracts with hospitals and specialists, and facilitate the collection and analysis of hospital and medical data as such, they are looking for a Data Engineer / Senior Technical BA Hybrid to join their IT team in Kew.

You’ll sit at the centre of their bespoke internal systems, 20 member health funds, external vendors and development teams, working across complex health datasets and integrations in a highly regulated environment.
  
Key Responsibilities:

Work across the full data delivery lifecycle – from requirements through to implementation
Lead stakeholder workshops, translating business problems into data-driven solutions
Act as the technical bridge between data sources, systems, and stakeholders
Design and document data flows, mappings, and transformations across multiple systems
Own data integration and migration activities (XML, JSON, CSV, TXT)
Perform hands-on data analysis using SQL across complex relational datasets
Support and enhance Power BI reporting (data modelling, DAX, dashboards)
Collaborate with engineering teams on API integrations and data pipelines (Azure environment)
Drive UAT and validation from a data integrity perspective
Identify opportunities to improve data quality, structure, and reporting capability
  
Key Requirements:

7-10+ years in a hands-on data-focused role (Data Engineer, Technical BA, Analytics Engineer)
Strong experience working with structured and semi-structured data (XML, JSON, CSV, TXT)
Advanced SQL skills – complex querying, data transformation, schema understanding
Solid experience in data modelling (star schemas, relationships, optimisation for reporting)
Proven experience with Power BI (data models, DAX, dashboard development)
Experience working on data integrations, APIs, and cloud platforms (Azure preferred)
Ability to translate between business needs and technical/data solutions
Exposure to healthcare datasets (HCP, MBS, DRG, ICD) – highly desirable but not mandatory if data skills are strong
Background in development (.NET, Angular) or engineering is a strong advantage
The Type of Person Who Will Thrive In the role

Someone who has come from a development or data-heavy background and naturally leans into the technical side of analysis.
You’re self-led. You don’t wait to be told what to do. You bring ideas to the table.
You’re not interested in simply ticking requirement boxes, you want to understand the “why”, challenge thinking where needed, and contribute to building better systems.
Most importantly, you want to be part of a growth journey, helping shape and strengthen AHSA’s technology landscape as it evolves.
  
If that sounds like you, we’d love to hear from you.
Our passion for people, technology, and commitment to being experts in our industry specializations is what sets us apart in the marketplace. 

For more information, please contact Bianca Rivera at bri••••@synchropartners.com.au

THE OPPORTUNITY

At SkinKandy, we are the piercing professionals, and the industry leader in body piercing and jewellery. Uniquely growing at a rapid rate, opening multiple new stores every year across Australia and New Zealand, with international expansion being the next chapter; this is the moment to join our dynamic business!

THE ROLE

We’re hiring an FP&A Manager to own the financial planning that is taking us through the next phase of growth. Reporting to the Head of Finance, you’ll work shoulder-to-shoulder with the CFO and have regular line of sight to the CEO.

This is a genuinely autonomous seat. You’ll own the models, the forecasts, the scenario work and the commercial narrative that sits behind them. It’s an opportunity where the quality of your thinking shows up in the decisions the business makes, new store economics, expansion geographies, capex prioritisation, pricing, and mix.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Own the financial models end-to-end: build, maintain and evolve the three-statement model, driver-based forecasts and the rolling re-forecast process.

Lead the annual budget and long-range planning cycles, including new store roll-out modelling, unit economics and payback analysis.

Build the reporting layer in Power BI on top of the data lake — turning raw transactional data into board-ready insight the exec team actually uses.

Partner with the CFO and Head of Finance on board materials, investor reporting and strategic analysis, including work that supports the group’s capital markets readiness.

Run scenario and sensitivity analysis on international expansion, new market entry, capex decisions and pricing/mix initiatives.

Be the trusted commercial sparring partner to operations, retail and marketing; translating the numbers into decisions and actions.

Drive continuous improvement across FP&A processes, reporting cadence and the underlying data model.

Set and report on labour and revenue targets and various other operational metrics

Continuous cost base analysis

Opportunities for business partnering projects

Ad-hoc and ever evolving reporting requirements depending on the needs of the business – no week will look the same as the last!

 ABOUT YOU

CA/CPA qualified (or equivalent) with a strong FP&A, commercial finance or investment banking background.

Exceptional financial modelling skills — you can build a driver-based model from a blank sheet and defend every line of it.

Comfortable working across a modern data stack — SQL and/or data lake exposure, and genuine fluency in Power BI (DAX confidence is a big plus).

Commercially curious. You ask why the numbers are what they are, not just what they are.

Experience in fast-growth, high-change environments. PE-backed, retail, multi-site or pre-IPO experience is highly regarded.

Self-directed and comfortable operating with autonomy at pace — this role suits someone who wants to build, not maintain.

Flexible in all aspects – if you are asked to dive into something new and without precedence, you will be someone who enjoys the experience and thrives in a problem solving environment.

LOCATION

This role is based onsite at SkinKandy’s HQ in Maroochydore on the Sunshine Coast. Sunshine, surf, and one of the best lifestyle-to-career trade-offs in the country; working in a business with genuine national and international ambition.

INTERESTED IN JOINING THE PIERCING PRO'S AND BE A PART OF ANZ'S FASTEST GROWING RETAILER?

We’d love to hear from you. Apply with your CV, or reach out for a confidential conversation to learn more about the role, the business and the journey ahead.

Job Title: Commercial Finance Transformation Lead (6 Month FTC)
Ad Text:
Western Sydney | Onsite Parking | Hybrid Working
6-Month Fixed Term Contract (Potential for Extension/Permanency)
Suitable for Commercially Minded Finance Leaders with Passion For Transformation
About the Company and Role
We are currently partnering a leading industrial manufacturing group  to recruit a Commercial Finance Transformation Lead on a 6-month fixed-term contract. This is a fantastic opportunity for a commercially minded finance leader with a passion for transformation, operating model design, and building best-in-class FP&A capability. This is a high-impact, hands-on role where you will play a critical part in shaping the Commercial Finance operating model, driving planning capability, and enabling better decision-making across the business.
  
Key Responsibilities

Design and implement the Commercial Finance operating model including FP&A and Business Partnering structures
Establish planning, forecasting, and performance management frameworks (budgeting, rolling forecasts, long-range planning)
Define and support implementation of systems, reporting, and planning tools
Partner with ERP and data teams to ensure fit-for-purpose financial systems and reporting capability
Drive stakeholder engagement and change management across divisional leadership teams
Support the rollout of new planning and reporting platforms
Build governance frameworks, reporting cadence, and KPI structures
Assist in recruitment and onboarding of the permanent Commercial Finance team
Develop and execute a transformation roadmap, tracking delivery against key milestones
  
About You
You will bring:

CA/CPA preferred with ~15+ years’ experience across commercial finance, FP&A, and/or transformation roles.
Hands-on finance transformation experience with ability to operate both strategically and tactically in complex environments
Strong commercial finance and business partnering capability
Experience implementing planning, forecasting, or ERP/reporting systems
Ability to navigate complex, multi-entity environments
Strong stakeholder engagement and influencing skills
Experience supporting team build-out and capability uplift
  
Why Apply?

Shape a finance function from the ground up
Work directly with an experienced and high-performing leadership team
Exposure to a large-scale, multi-entity transformation
A fast-paced, high-impact environment where your work will deliver tangible outcomes
Potential for contract extension based on delivery milestones

How to Apply:
To apply, please click on the appropriate link below. Alternatively, for a confidential discussion, please contact Samantha Tan at sta•@sharpandcarter.com.au

The Company
Group FP&A Manager required for a large, well established Australian organisation with a significant national footprint. The business operates across a complex multi business unit structure and is currently strengthening its central finance function to support Group level planning, performance management and strategic decision making.
 
The Role
Reporting into the Senior Group FP&A Manager, you will sit within a centralised finance team responsible for delivering high quality Group reporting, planning and analysis. You will work closely with senior stakeholders across multiple business units, providing insight and challenge to support both operational performance and longer term strategic initiatives.
 
Your responsibilities will include:

Ownership and coordination of the Group five year planning process across P&L, balance sheet and cash flow
Delivery of Group budgeting, forecasting and monthly performance reporting cycles
Preparation of executive level and board reporting packs with clear commentary and insights
Development of financial models, scenario analysis and long range planning outputs
Support for investment evaluation, capital allocation and wider commercial decision making
Involvement in acquisition, divestment and due diligence activity as required
Business partnering with senior stakeholders across multiple functions to challenge assumptions and drive performance improvement
Ongoing development and enhancement of planning tools, reporting frameworks and finance systems
About You
We are looking for a qualified finance professional with strong experience in Group FP&A or commercial finance within complex, multi entity organisations. You will bring:

A recognised accounting qualification such as CA or CPA
Strong experience in Group level planning, forecasting and reporting
Advanced financial modelling capability across P&L, balance sheet and cash flow
Exposure to investment analysis, M&A or capital projects
Ideally experience with enterprise planning systems (TM1 or similar)
Strong stakeholder management skills with the ability to influence senior non finance audiences
A commercial, analytical and detail driven mindset with strong problem solving capability
How to Apply
If you are interested in hearing more, please apply via the link or email your resume to Lewis Black at lbl•••@fourquarters.com.au

Company Information

Global technology business that continues to thrive and grow at a rapid rate. This is a unique and world leading innovative business. They operate in over forty countries and continue to broaden scope.

The business is highly progressive and dynamic. The team you will work with our talented, driven and proud of the business they impact on every day. The feedback from staff that work for this business is that the culture is outstanding. They offer a very flexible hybrid work model and are driven by outcomes and performance.

Responsibilities

The role reports to the APAC President and works closely with all Business Unit leaders. You will get the opportunity to work across multiple regions in APAC. The role will combine business intelligence, commercial analysis, and market strategy support, acting as a key advisor to senior leadership across the region.

The key focus areas of the role are -

Marketing & Revenue Analytics - Analyse marketing performance, track effectiveness of marketing spend and recommend data-driven improvements

Sales Performance Analysis - Monitor and analyse sales performance across APAC by region, industry, product, and salesperson. Develop dashboards to track performance and opportunities

Sales Campaign Monitoring - Track effectiveness of sales campaigns and initiatives. Provide insights into campaign performance and recommend optimisation strategies

Market & Industry Intelligence - Conduct market sizing, competitive analysis, and industry landscape research for new business opportunities. Engage with industry stakeholders and external data sources to support strategic planning

Executive & Strategic Support - Deliver ad hoc analysis and strategic insights to APAC President and senior leadership team. Support decision-making through data storytelling and commercial recommendations

Product & Platform Analytics - Collaborate with the Product Manager to analyse product sales performance. Identify product-level opportunities and performance gaps

Salesforce & BI Enablement – Analysis of data to identify trends and identify opportunities

Background

4 + years experience in Business Intelligence, Commercial Analytics, Strategy, or Revenue Operations type roles

Strong Salesforce reporting and analytics experience

Advanced Excel skills. Experience with BI Tools (Power BI, Tableau etc)

Strong analytical skills and ability to translate data into actionable business insights

Experience working in an international (multiple country) role

Experience in B2B SaaS, technology, security, or industrial sectors would be advantageous but not essential

Management consulting or corporate strategy background is also advantageous but not essential

Ability to translate data into business decisions rather than reporting alone

Benefits

New role due to sustained growth

Partner with senior stakeholders and make an impact

Highly commercial, analytical and data driven role

Terrific culture, great leadership and a collaborative team

A unique and highly successful global business

Hybrid role - they are outcome driven rather than location of work. Work from home 2 + days per week

As this role looks after four regions you will have the opportunity to travel to your locations (about 20% of the time)

Excellent salary package 150k to 160k + super

Career progression as the company and role evolves

If you are interested in this role and have the suitable experience please apply.

Exciting opportunity to join an organisation undergoing significant expansion across its operating asset portfolio.

With continued investment in systems, infrastructure and operational growth, the business is seeking a commercially minded Finance Systems Analyst who can bridge the gap between finance, technology and operations.

This is a highly visible role suited to someone who enjoys solving complex business problems, building scalable financial processes and partnering with senior leadership to support strategic decision-making.

Working closely with a variety of stakeholders, you will play a critical role in improving reporting capability, integrating systems and supporting the financial modelling and setup of newly acquired or established business units.

The Opportunity
